reasoning: The table name interpolated into wpdb->prepare() is provably constant: $wpdb->prefix is WordPress-controlled (set in wp-config.php by an admin) and 'icl_translations' is a hardcoded string literal. No attacker can influence $tranlationsTable. The $postType and $taxonomy parameters are sanitized with sanitize_key() before use as %s placeholders, adding defense-in-depth. All three call sites in the plugin pass either hardcoded strings ('product_cat') or default values ('product') — no call site reads from $_GET, $_POST, $_REQUEST, or any other user-supplied source. $postID and $termID are integers from WordPress object constructors (absint() applied) used as %d placeholders. The phpcs suppression comments mask the pattern from automated tooling, which is a valid maintainability observation, but there is no exploitable SQL injection path. Verdict: code quality concern only, not a real exploitable vulnerability.
